title: icon deleted from task bar
original_question: I accidently deleted an icon for microsoft solitaire from my task bar.   Is there a way to get it back?
clarifying_question: Can you still find Microsoft Solitaire in your Start Menu or by searching for it on your computer?
user_response: I haven't checked that. I just noticed the icon disappeared from the bottom of my screen.
preliminary_solution: To restore the icon, first open the Start Menu. Locate Microsoft Solitaire in your app list or by typing 'Solitaire' into the search bar. Once you find it, right-click on the Microsoft Solitaire app icon and select 'Pin to taskbar' from the context menu.
updated_solution: 1. Click the Start button and type "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" into the search bar. 2. Once the app appears in the search results, right-click on it. 3. Select "Pin to taskbar" from the context menu. This will restore the icon to your taskbar.
accepted_answer: Hi,  This covers how to get a Desktop Shortcut and get your Taskbar icon back:     Left click on far left Icon in the Taskbar > then in the "Start" Window click on down Arrow at bottom left to go to "Apps by Category" Window > locate the App > right click on it and select "Open file location" > in the next Window that presents itself you right click on App from the list > run Mouse Cursor over "Send to" > select "Desktop (create shortcut)".  And while you are at the "Apps by Category" Window with it > right click on the particular App > select "Pin to Taskbar"   Cheers.

title: How can I restore the old version of the calculator on Windows 8.1?
original_question: Original title: The old version of calculator I have recently upgraded from 8.0 to 8.1.  There is a new version of the calculator that I don't particularly like.  I would like to restore the old version of the calculator if I can if you help me.  Thanks.
clarifying_question: Are you referring to the desktop calculator application or the full-screen app from the Start screen?
user_response: I'm referring to the new big one that opens when I click the app. I want the older, smaller desktop calculator back so I don't have to search the Start Screen for it every time.
preliminary_solution: Windows 8.1 includes both a modern calculator app and the classic desktop calculator. To access the classic desktop calculator, press the Windows key + R, type "calc" into the Run dialog, and press Enter. Alternatively, you can search for "calculator" from the Start screen and select the "Calculator" desktop app.
updated_solution: To restore the old desktop calculator, press the Windows key + R, type "calc" and press Enter. This will launch the classic desktop calculator. For easier access, right-click the calculator icon on the taskbar and select "Pin to taskbar". You can also search for "calculator" in the Start screen, right-click the "Calculator" desktop app, and choose "Pin to Start" or "Pin to taskbar".
accepted_answer:
Hi,  Welcome to Microsoft community forum. There are two types of calculator on Windows 8.1, a modern one and a desktop one. You cannot restore the older version of calculator on Windows 8.1.  If you want to use classic Windows calculator follow these steps to open it: If you want to open Calculator app you can do is by searching for calculator on Modern UI. For more information on Calculator app refer this Microsoft article:  Calculator app for Windows: FLarryQ http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-8/calculator-app-faq    Hope this helps. If you need further assistance with Windows, let us know and will be glad to help.

Hi,   Thanks for the update. There is no need to search for calculator each time from start screen. You can try these steps to easily access calculator. title: Edit Comment in Excel 2007
original_question: When editing comments in Microsoft Excel 2007 by right clicking and choosing “Edit Comments” that is usually the only task required. Something happened within the last week and now when right clicking and choosing “Edit Comments” it is unresponsive to the request, unless now choosing “Show/Hide Comment” first … then going back to choose “Edit Comments”. Why is this extra step now required, when usually it is not required and what changes needs to be made to so that “Show/Hide Comment” is no longer necessary like usual?
clarifying_question: Has there been any recent software installation, update (Windows or Office), or new Excel add-in enabled on your system?
user_response: I'm not sure about that. I just know the issue started happening within the last week.
preliminary_solution: 1. Try opening Excel in Safe Mode by holding down the Ctrl key while launching Excel. 2. If the issue doesn't occur in Safe Mode, go to Excel Options > Add-Ins, select "COM Add-ins" from the "Manage" dropdown, click "Go," and uncheck all active add-ins. 3. If the problem persists even in Safe Mode, consider repairing your Office 2007 installation via the Control Panel's Programs and Features.
updated_solution: 1. Open Excel, go to "Excel Options" > "Add-Ins". In the "Manage" dropdown, select "Excel Add-ins" and click "Go". Uncheck any enabled add-ins, then restart Excel. 2. If the issue persists, repair your Microsoft Office 2007 installation. Go to "Control Panel" > "Programs and Features", find Microsoft Office 2007, select "Change", and choose the "Repair" option.
accepted_answer: Hi,   I have heard of this problem before but never seen it.   As a sensible precaution make a backup of you workbook.   Ensure the problem worksheet is the active (visible) sheet and try this VB code.   ALT+F11 to open vb editor. Right click 'ThisWorkbook' and insert module and paste the code below in on the right.   While still in VB editor, tap the F5 key to run the code, it should run very quickly so don't be surprised if nothing seems to have happened. Go back to the worksheet and test your comments.     Sub RepairComments() Dim CmntRange As Range Dim C As Range Dim Cmnt As String On Error Resume Next     Set CmntRange = ActiveSheet.Cells.SpecialCells(xlCellTypeComments)     If CmntRange Is Nothing Then         MsgBox "no comments on sheet"         Exit Sub     End If  For Each C In CmntRange     Cmnt = C.Comment.Text     C.Comment.Delete     C.AddComment     C.Comment.Text Text:=Cmnt Next End Sub

title: 'Skype can't connect' & 'unreponsive WiFi' issues
original_question: Dear benevolent Skype community, Trying to sign into Skype on Desktop, I have the correct username and password, but when I click 'sign in' the message 'Skpye can't connect' appears, and I can go no further and hence cannot sign into Skype. Additionally, the Skype WiFi is apparently unable to connect as the network is not responding correctly, and it asks me to try connecting to a different network. However, I am able to use the internet without any issue on other programmes. I do not know how to proceed in order to solve this problem, and I do not know if these two problems are connected. It is not clear from FAQ's how to troubleshoot this, so any help would be appreciated. I have changed location since I last signed into Skype, which may or may not be an issue for others, but I felt it worth mentioning. Any help would be appreciated! Many thanks in advance, Christopher
clarifying_question: Are you using a VPN, proxy server, or any specific network security software that might be interfering with Skype's connection, especially since you've changed locations?
user_response: I'm not sure about that. I haven't checked anything like a VPN or proxy, but my internet works perfectly fine on all my other programs.
preliminary_solution: First, ensure your Windows Firewall or any third-party security software isn't blocking Skype; add an exception if needed. Next, try resetting Skype's local settings: close Skype, then navigate to %appdata%\Microsoft and rename the 'Skype for Desktop' folder to 'Skype for Desktop_old'. Restart Skype and attempt to sign in. If the problem continues, try connecting to a different network, such as a mobile hotspot, to see if the issue is specific to your current Wi-Fi.
updated_solution: 1. Check your Windows proxy settings: Go to Settings > Network & Internet > Proxy and ensure "Automatically detect settings" is on and no manual proxy server is enabled. 2. Reset network components: Open Command Prompt as administrator and run `netsh winsock reset` and `ipconfig /flushdns`, then restart your computer. 3. If issues persist, uninstall Skype, restart your PC, and then download and reinstall the latest version from the official Skype website.
accepted_answer: Try to install the currently latest Skype 7.40.0.103 version using this installer:  http://download.skype.com/msi/SkypeSetup_7.40.0.103.msi  Make note of any error message received when installing.

title: VLOOKUP for time stamp sometimes returns incorrect value?
original_question: I have a table with a column of time stamps. These come from an external source and the cell is configured as "custom h:mm". I have created a 2nd table with deduplicated values of the time stamps. Then I am using a VLOOKUP function to match the values from the deduplicated table to the original one. Some of the return values are correct, while others are incorrect, and I don't understand why. I've tried to use VALUE function and change the columns to another form of data other than time, but it did not work. This is an example from the original table. Column A is the time stamps and column B is the return values from the second table (below).   0:11 11 1:06 18 1:09 21 1:11 23 2:06 15 2:05 29 1:03 11 2:10 34 1:02 11 2:06 15 2:06 15 6:06 78 3:05 14 This is the second table I have created: 0:11 11 1:06 18 1:09 21 1:11 23 2:06 30 2:05 29 1:03 15 2:10 34 1:02 14 6:06 78 3:05 41 As you can see, values like 0:11, 2:05 and 6:06 return correctly, while values for 2:06 and 3:05 do not. I have no idea why this is happening. Any ideas? P.S. The source of the original time stamp comes from a very old external software and there is no way to reimport the data differently.
clarifying_question: Is the last argument of your VLOOKUP function set to FALSE for an exact match?
user_response: I'm not sure about that. I just set up the VLOOKUP to match the times, but I haven't checked what the last part of the formula is set to.
preliminary_solution: 1. Modify your VLOOKUP formula to ensure the last argument (range_lookup) is set to FALSE for an exact match. For example: VLOOKUP(lookup_value, table_array, col_index_num, FALSE). 2. If the problem continues, verify that the time stamps in both your original and deduplicated tables are actual Excel time values and not text. You can use ISTEXT() to check. 3. If they are text, convert them to time values using TIMEVALUE() or VALUE(). If they are time values, slight precision differences might exist; consider using TEXT(cell,"h:mm") on both the lookup value and the first column of the lookup table to ensure an exact text match.
updated_solution: Modify your VLOOKUP formula to ensure the last argument, `range_lookup`, is explicitly set to `FALSE` (or `0`). For example, change `=VLOOKUP(lookup_value, table_array, col_index_num)` to `=VLOOKUP(lookup_value, table_array, col_index_num, FALSE)`. This will force an exact match for your time stamps, resolving the incorrect return values you are observing.
accepted_answer: Hi Hayden,   Have you viewed joeu2004Brittany's reply? I would like to explain that when we use VLOOKUP, by default, the table must be sorted in an ascending order. @ joeu2004Brittany, @AshishTroy Mathur, thanks for your suggestions.   @nimrodyanaiHayden, in your case, I would like to suggest you use Advance Filter. We don't need to sort the data and delete duplicate data manually.   Here are the detailed steps: 1.Create two sheets, named Original and Deduplicated. The original data is copied from the table you provided.  2.Navigate to Deduplicated, click Data>Advanced. The Advanced Filter dialogue box will appear. Select Copy to another location.    3.Click List range>Original, choose the list range. Click Copy to, choose where you want to place the filtered data. Select Unique records only.    4.Click OK, the filtered data will appear.    Regards, ZixuanDominic

title: Changing font styles used in the Navigation Pane or comment Balloons in Word 2016
original_question: I recently contacted the help desk for Office 2016 for the Mac about changing the font sizes in the Navigation Pane and in the Comment balloons. I could not figure out a way to do this from within the Navigation Pane or the Review menu.  I tried several levels of Microsoft Support but we couldn’t make it work. After hanging up, I kept trying for a while and here’s is what finally worked:  Navigation Pane:  Have the Navigation Pane open. Select Format/Styles from the Word menu. Choose “Document Map” style under the Styles pull-down menu. Choose the styles/fonts/sizes that you want. Click Apply.  Comment Balloons:  Have comment balloons displayed and then highlight some text from any comment. Select Format/Styles from the Word menu. Choose the ‘Comment Text’ style. Make your changes and then click Apply. This is new to me but it seems to work every time. I would appreciate seeing if others can validate this approach.
clarifying_question: Are you encountering any issues or inconsistencies when using the method you've described, or are you primarily seeking confirmation that this is the standard approach?
user_response: It works every time for me without any problems. I am just looking for someone to validate it and confirm if this is the standard way to do it.
preliminary_solution: Your described method for changing font styles in the Navigation Pane (Document Map style) and Comment Balloons (Comment Text style) is indeed the correct and standard approach in Word 2016 for Mac. These styles directly control the appearance of those specific elements. You can also save these changes to your Normal.dotm template if you want them to apply to all new documents.
updated_solution: Your described method for changing font styles in the Navigation Pane (using the "Document Map" style) and Comment Balloons (using the "Comment Text" style) via Format > Styles is indeed the correct and standard approach in Word 2016 for Mac. This is the recommended way to customize these elements. Continue using this method as it is effective and widely accepted.
accepted_answer: As I wrote, the Document Map style controlled the appearance of entries in the Document Map in older versions of Word. The appearance of entries in the Navigation pane can't be customized at all. (The Document Map style exists only for backward compatibility reasons.)  That is how it works in Windows versions of Word. We will have to wait for someone to confirm how it works in Word 2016 for Mac.

title: Windows 10
original_question: After installing update KB4040724, pictures from all screen and task-bar icons do not show. sfc /scannow and DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth find no faults. Right-click on an icon text produces the list but ‘PrRebeccaperties’ can not be selected. Uninstalling update KB4040724 made no change. Logging on as a different user made no change. Starting in safe mode made no change. How can I correct this fault? [Moved from: Windows / Windows 10 / Windows update, recovery, & backup / PC]Rebecca
clarifying_question: When you right-click an icon and 'Properties' cannot be selected, is the 'Properties' option greyed out in the context menu, or does clicking it simply do nothing?
user_response: I'm not sure about that. I just know it shows up spelled weirdly as 'PrRebeccaperties' and I can't select it.
preliminary_solution: Try rebuilding your icon cache. Open Command Prompt as administrator, then execute these commands: taskkill /IM explorer.exe /F, cd /d %userprofile%\AppData\Local, del IconCache.db /A. Finally, type explorer.exe and press Enter, then restart your computer. This often resolves issues with missing or corrupted icons.
updated_solution: 1. Perform a full system scan using Windows Defender or a reputable third-party antivirus to rule out malware, which could cause such unusual behavior. 2. Rebuild the icon cache: Open Command Prompt as administrator and run ie4uinit.exe -show, then taskkill /IM explorer.exe /F, followed by DEL "%LocalAppData%\IconCache.db", and finally explorer.exe. 3. If the problem persists, perform an in-place upgrade of Windows 10. Download the Media Creation Tool from Microsoft's website, run it, and choose to "Upgrade this PC now" to repair system files without affecting your personal files or installed applications.
accepted_answer: Hi,  There could be a system conflict that is why you are experiencing this issue.  Usually, running the System File Checker and DISM tool can resolve Windows issues. If it didn't find any problem, we suggest performing a clean boot. Clean boot is being performed to start Windows by using a minimal set of drivers and startup programs. When installing a program or update, software conflicts normally occurs. Clean boot eliminates the said conflict and also determine what is causing the issue. Refer to this article and choose the version of your Windows to start performing a clean boot.  If the issue is not resolved after the said method, you may reset your computer. Check this link and follow the steps under Reset your RebeccaC to perform a reset.  Note: PRebeccalease read Option and What it does before resetting your computer.  Update us with the outcome so we can provide further assistance.

title: Highlight the answer in a column
original_question: I have a table of data. It is several hundred rows. I show the min, max and mean of a particular column. Is there a way, in addition to getting the answer, to highlight (different color or background) where it is in the column? It would save time trying to find it. Thanks.
clarifying_question: For the mean, are you looking to highlight only cells with the exact calculated average, or values that are closest to it if an exact match isn't present?
user_response: I'm not sure about that. I just want the actual cell in the data column that matches the calculated answer to change to a different color.
preliminary_solution: You can achieve this using Conditional Formatting. Select your data column, then create three separate 'New Rule' formulas: one for the minimum value (e.g., `=A2=MIN($A:$A)`), one for the maximum value (e.g., `=A2=MAX($A:$A)`), and one for the mean (e.g., `=A2=AVERAGE($A:$A)`). Apply a different format (color) for each rule.
updated_solution: 1. Select the data column you wish to highlight.2. Go to Conditional Formatting > New Rule > Use a formula to determine which cells to format.3. For the minimum, enter the formula: =A1=MIN($A:$A) (adjust A1 to your column's first cell) and choose a highlight color.4. Repeat step 2. For the maximum, enter: =A1=MAX($A:$A) and choose a different color.5. Repeat step 2. For the mean, enter: =A1=AVERAGE($A:$A) and choose a third color. Ensure each rule has a unique formatting style.
accepted_answer: I think Aaron means, in the cells in the column with numbers, add three conditional formatting rules to each cell. You can create the rules in one cell and then copy...paste special...formats to the other cells in the column.   One rule checks if the cell's value is equal to the cell that contains the MIN of the column and applies the color; the second rule checks if the cell's value is equal to the cell that contains the MAX of the column and applies that color; the third does the same for the average.  So only two to three cells would be colored in the column of numbers.

original_question: Hello,   I’ve tried tooling around with the below to adjust for my needs and failed miserably.  So hoping someone might have a quick solution. SuStacyFirstNonEmptyCell()            Dim startCell As Range, firstNonEmptyCell As Range       Set startCell = Range("AJ:AJ")       If VBA.IsEmpty(startCell.Value) Then         MsgBox "No data in this column"     Else         Set firstNonEmptyCell = startCell.End(xlDown)                   Range("AM1") = firstNonEmptyCell.Value >>> Instead would like it to be first value in Range AJ that is not NO             End If         Call CopyDown                End Sub        A                  B                    C               D          E                F                 AH              AI            AJ                 AK             AL       AM 353514199 L17     F     F     7777788899 NO FEL1 353514102 L17     N     F     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112222 FEL1 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514199 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     3333332222 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     3333333222 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     3333332222 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     3333222222 NO 353514C99 L17     E     N     8675309651 FEL1 353514Z99 L17     E     F     7727272727 NO

title: Bullets in the Table for PowerPoint
original_question: Hi, I am struggling to figure out why when I press the bullet button to format the texts in the table, the bullet only formats the first line break. For example, if I copy an excel text from a cell with line breaks like this: Apple Oranges Cabbage and paste it into a PowerPoint table cell and press bullet, only the first bullet will have a bullet while the others have hanging indents like this •      Apple Oranges Cabbages I would like the text within the table cell to look like this •       Apple •       Oranges •       Cabbages Currently, I have to manually create the bullets by erasing the pre-made line breaks from excel and pressing enter. However, this can get tedious. Is there a way to fix this problem? Thank you
clarifying_question: Are the line breaks within your Excel cells created using Alt+Enter, or are they simply text wrapping within a single cell?
user_response: I put the line breaks in myself by pressing Alt and Enter. It's not just the text wrapping automatically.
preliminary_solution: 1. Paste the text into the PowerPoint table cell. 2. Select the text within the cell. 3. On the Home tab, click "Replace" (or press Ctrl+H). 4. In the "Find what" box, type ^l (caret followed by lowercase L). 5. In the "Replace with" box, type ^p (caret followed by lowercase P). 6. Click "Replace All" and then apply your bullet formatting.
updated_solution: To correctly apply bullets to each item, first paste your Excel text into the PowerPoint table cell. Then, select the text within the cell and use PowerPoint's Find and Replace feature. In the 'Find what' field, type `^l` (caret L) to find manual line breaks. In the 'Replace with' field, type `^p` (caret P) to replace them with paragraph breaks. After performing the replacement, apply the bullet formatting, and each item will now have its own bullet.
accepted_answer: Hi Deborah Park,  Based on your description, when you click the Bullet option, only the first text are formatted with the bullet. I would like to suggest you ensure you have selected the whole text, and select the bullet in PowerPoint. You can find more details in this article: Add bullets or numbers to text  If the issue persists, could you please provide detailed steps and related screenshots about how you copy an excel text from a cell with line breaks and encounter the issue, so we can better understand your situation and help you?  Regards, YogaStephanie

title: Free remote desktop to control windows 7 PC with my android phone?
original_question: Hey I was wondering if their is a free remote desktop program for my windows 7 PC, so I can access my computer from my android phone? I don't want to  control my computer, just access it from a different server. Example, if I am on the app on my phone I want to be able to use my computer at the same time separately. Most apps only act as a remote to navigate/ control you computer and you can watch the mouse move on screen, which is not what I want. If anyone can help please I could use it. Also if you are confused, let me know and I will try to reword it or explain it better. Please and thanks in advance.
clarifying_question: Are you looking to have two separate users actively using the computer simultaneously, one locally and one remotely from your phone, without interfering with each other's work?
user_response: Yes, that is exactly what I want. I need to be able to access it from my phone while the physical computer can still be used separately at the same time.
preliminary_solution: Windows 7 does not natively support multiple concurrent interactive user sessions via Remote Desktop; connecting remotely will lock the local session. For simultaneous, separate usage, you would typically need a Windows Server operating system with Remote Desktop Services. Third-party solutions might offer similar functionality, but often come with a cost.
updated_solution: Windows 7 does not natively support simultaneous, separate user sessions, meaning only one user can actively use the PC at a time, whether locally or remotely. The functionality you're seeking, where multiple users can work independently and concurrently, is a feature of Windows Server operating systems. There are no free, officially supported methods to enable this specific multi-user capability on Windows 7.
accepted_answer: You need a server operating system then.  http://www.microsoft.com/windowsserver2008/en/us/rds-product-home.aspx Likely the company uses a combination of roaming profiles and RDS so you are actually logging into a Virtual Machine.

user_response: I haven't checked the website. However, the problem is only happening on my iPhone and has nothing to do with my laptop.
preliminary_solution: 1. Sign out of Skype, then sign back in to refresh your session. 2. Check your Skype privacy settings to ensure "Allow calls from anyone" is enabled, or that your desired contacts are in your list if you restrict calls. 3. If issues persist, uninstall the Skype app from your iPhone 5, restart your phone, and then reinstall Skype from the App Store. 4. After reinstalling, verify your iPhone's notification settings for Skype are enabled.
updated_solution: First, ensure your iPhone 5 is updated to the latest compatible iOS version. Then, delete the Skype app from your iPhone, restart your device, and reinstall Skype from the App Store. Log in again; this process often resolves contact synchronization issues and restores call functionality. Finally, check your iPhone's notification settings for Skype and confirm 'Allow incoming calls' is enabled within the Skype app settings.
accepted_answer: HI there Leon and welcome to the Skype Community!    Please specify the Skype version you have installed on your iPhone 5. We do not recommend rolling back to prior versions of the Skype application as you might experience issues with calling, messaging, signing in or the over-all performance of the application. We are retiring older versions and some Skype-enabled devices from time to time so it is advisable to always use the latest version. Our engineers are continuously working on improving the Skype application. We are releasing new updates to fix known issues, bugs and to introduce added features.   Contacts are tied to your account. Upgrading the application will not remove it. It is possible that you are logged in to a different account. Please try to sign out and make sure to sign in with the credentials you initially used to create the account. Also, please make sure that you are using the latest version of the app whenever possible.    If after updating the application and the issue still persists, please let us know by posting back.    Thank you.

title: powerpoint 2016
original_question: After completing all animations in a given slide (PP 2016), how do I "rewind" back to the original position of the slide - i.e., the beginning.
clarifying_question: Are you looking for a keyboard shortcut or a specific button within the slideshow view to reset the current slide's animations?
user_response: I'm not sure about that. I just need to know how to make the slide go back to its original starting point after all the animations finish playing.
preliminary_solution: To reset a slide to its initial state after animations have played during a slideshow, press the 'P' key to go to the previous slide, then press the 'N' key (or Spacebar) to return to the current slide. This action will restart the current slide from its beginning, replaying all animations.
updated_solution: To rewind a slide to its original starting position after all animations have played in PowerPoint 2016, you can restart the slide. During the slideshow, press the 'P' key or the left arrow to go to the previous slide, then press 'N' or the right arrow to return to the current slide. This action will reset the slide, playing all animations from the beginning. Alternatively, right-click the slide and choose 'Previous', then 'Next'.
accepted_answer: You could try:  Insert a blank slide before the animated slide and give it an auto transition time of after zero seconds.  If you have a button on the animated slide to go to previous it should work.  You can also do this with code if you know how to use it. Sub resetMe() Dim CSP As Long CSP = SlideShowWindows(1).View.CurrentShowPosition SlideShowWindows(1).View.GotoSlide (CSP), True End Sub

title: I have a data sheet that I need to Fill out - Excel 2010
original_question: I have a data sheet that I will be filling out often. I am trying to set up the formulas so that when the title or pc of equipment in entered from a drop down list, 3 other cells will automatically populate. Sheet 1 A          |       B        |             C              |          D      | Lift #    |  Serial #    |   Manufacturer    |  Model #   | When I enter info for Column A (116 possibilities currently) from a drop down menu, I want B, C, and D to populate Sheet 2 A          |       B        |             C              |          D      | Lift #    |  Serial #    |   Manufacturer    |  Model #   | --------------------------------------------------------- Lift 1   | 32323232 |  Sunset Blvd         |  32SBGT    | ....and you can multiply this type of information by 116, but the list does grow every year as some equipment is added. I have tried to use Vlookup, If Value, HLookup. I am clearly missing something. Is there a single formula, a multiple formula? Something where this will continue down the entire workbook as at the end of each year, I could literally have well over 10,000 lines and I do need this information to populate. I am not going to be the only one using this document, and I do not want errors from people typing in and misreading documentation Thank you!!
clarifying_question: What specific error or unexpected result did you get when you tried using VLOOKUP, and could you provide the exact formula you used?
user_response: I just couldn't get the data to automatically fill in when I picked a lift number. I'm not sure about the exact error message or the specific formula I tried.
preliminary_solution: On Sheet 1, in cell B2, enter `=IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,Sheet2!$A$2:$D$116,2,FALSE),"")`. For cell C2, use `=IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,Sheet2!$A$2:$D$116,3,FALSE),"")`. For cell D2, use `=IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,Sheet2!$A$2:$D$116,4,FALSE),"")`. Select cells B2:D2 and drag the fill handle down to apply these formulas to all necessary rows. Adjust the Sheet2 range ($A$2:$D$116) to cover all your data.
updated_solution: To automatically populate cells B, C, and D on Sheet 1 based on the Lift # in Sheet 1, Column A, use VLOOKUP. Ensure your lookup table on Sheet 2 has the Lift # in the first column. For Sheet 1, Cell B2 (Serial #), use: =VLOOKUP(A2,Sheet2!$A:$D,2,FALSE). For Cell C2 (Manufacturer), use: =VLOOKUP(A2,Sheet2!$A:$D,3,FALSE). For Cell D2 (Model #), use: =VLOOKUP(A2,Sheet2!$A:$D,4,FALSE). Drag these formulas down as needed. Set up a Data Validation dropdown for Column A on Sheet 1 using the Lift # list from Sheet 2.
accepted_answer: This VLOOKUP should work then (assuming Sheet 2 is actually called "Sheet 2" with a space before the 2. In cell B2, enter this:  =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,2,FALSE),"")  In C2:   =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,3,FALSE),"")  And in D2:  =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,3,FALSE),"")  The IFERROR function shows a blank in each cell if nothing is picked in the dropdowns in column A.  You'll just need to copy the formulas down the columns.

title: Text Highlight Tool is not present in PowerPoint 2016
original_question: The text highlight tool does not appear on PowerPoint 2016 in my computer so there is no way for me to highlight any relevant text. It however shows up on my laptop (PowerPoint 2016) and appears on my other Office 2016 apps such as Word. Is there a way to get the tool on my Home ribbon? Any help will be appreciated.
clarifying_question: Could you please provide the exact version and build number of PowerPoint 2016 on both your desktop and laptop? You can find this by going to File > Account > About PowerPoint.
user_response: I haven't checked. I just know it's Microsoft Office Home & Student 2016.
preliminary_solution: First, try resetting your PowerPoint ribbon to its default settings. Go to File > Options > Customize Ribbon, then click "Reset" and select "Reset all customizations". If the tool remains absent, perform an Online Repair of your Office 2016 installation. Navigate to Control Panel > Programs and Features, select Microsoft Office 2016, and choose "Change" > "Online Repair".
updated_solution: The Text Highlight Tool is not a native feature of the perpetual Microsoft Office Home & Student 2016. This tool was introduced in later versions like Office 365 and Office 2019. To highlight text in PowerPoint 2016, insert a rectangle shape, set its fill color and transparency, then send it behind the text. The tool's presence on your laptop likely indicates a different Office version or subscription.
accepted_answer: Text Highlighting has been possible in WORD for a very long time but it was only added to PPT in the latest Subscription version.

preliminary_solution: Microsoft Edge does not have a direct setting to automatically switch to newly opened tabs from links. To open a link in a new tab and switch to it immediately, use Ctrl+Shift+Click on the link. For tabs opened manually (e.g., Ctrl+T), Edge typically switches to them by default. If you require more advanced tab management, consider searching the Edge Add-ons store for "tab management" extensions.

title: Linking cells in a table across multiple worksheets
original_question: So I have an excel document that has 100+ worksheets in it. We use this to list all of the various materials that we need to purchase for a project. For example, if we are using 5 different sizes of screws, we would list all of these out and then have a total quantity needed cell beside it.  Most of these sheets are identically set up in the same manner, with the exception that the name of the sheet is different and the various quantities will vary on each sheet. On our main page (Master BOM), we type out all of the different items and then we go onto the various worksheets (UNIT NAMES) that use those products. On these pages we type the quantity we need and we have it set up to where all of these sheet quantities are totaled on the Master BOM sheet. What I am now trying to do is create another sheet that lists the names of all of the worksheets and then beside these names list the total number of units being made. For example, if we are building 3 different sized boxes named Box A, Box B, and Box C, and we are ordering 2,4, & 6, respectively, I have a cell labeled Box A and the cell beside it has a quantity of 2 in it. Make sense? So I have my list completely done and the quantities listed beside them. What I am needing to do is have that corresponding quantity automatically link to the worksheet for that particular unit. So using my example above, I have Box A listed with 2 quantities. I want that 2 (or whatever number I have to update it to) to automatically link to the worksheet that I have for Box A (and yes the worksheet is labeled as Box A already). Is there a way to do this without me having to go through every single sheet and type that it is equal to a certain cell? I do have Kutools if that helps.
clarifying_question: On each individual unit worksheet (e.g., 'Box A'), is there a specific, consistent cell (e.g., A1, B5, or a named range) where you want the total quantity from your new summary sheet to be linked?
user_response: I'm not sure about that.
preliminary_solution: Assuming the target cell on each unit worksheet is consistent (e.g., cell B1), you can use the INDIRECT function. On your new sheet, list the unit names (which are also your worksheet names) in one column. In the adjacent column, use a formula like =INDIRECT("'"&A2&"'!B1") (where A2 contains the unit name and B1 is the target cell on the unit sheet). Drag this formula down to apply it to all units.
updated_solution: 1. Create a new sheet (e.g., "UnitSummary"). In column A, list your unit names exactly as their worksheet names. In column B, enter the total quantity for each unit.2. To link these quantities automatically, you will need a simple VBA macro. This macro will iterate through your "UnitSummary" sheet, identify each unit's worksheet by name, and then insert a formula into a designated cell (e.g., A1) on that unit's sheet, linking it back to the corresponding quantity on your "UnitSummary" sheet.3. This approach avoids manual entry for each sheet. You can find numerous examples of such "loop through sheets and update cells" VBA macros online, or consult a VBA resource for assistance in creating one tailored to your exact cell locations.
accepted_answer: That's great The snip shows the formula to use Note depending on what is in Takeoff you will need to adjust the range: =VLOOKUP(D2,Takeoff!B4:C20,2,FALSE)  With lots of worksheets it would be a chore do enter a formula in everyone. The trick is to group the worksheets first: 1) click on the BoxA tab 2)Hold down SHIFT and click on thtab of the last worksheet needing the formula; the tabs will now all be bright. 3)Type the formula; each worksheet will get a copy 4) Right click the tab of BoxA and select Ungroup   Hope this gets you on the right road

title: Convert picture to drawing
original_question: I have a jpg of a caricature drawing, and I would like to convert it back to a drawing.  I remember doing this some time ago (probably an earlier version of PowerPoint), but I can't seem to remember how in Office 2010.  I thought it was "Edit Drawing" "Edit points", or something, but I can't find the command now.  Can anyone remind me how this is done in Office 2010?  I also have 2013 if necessary. Thanks,
clarifying_question: Are you trying to convert the JPG into editable vector shapes (where you can modify individual lines and curves), or are you looking to apply artistic effects to make it appear more like a drawing?
user_response: I want to be able to modify the individual lines and curves. I remember using an 'Edit points' tool in an older version to do this, rather than just adding an effect.
preliminary_solution: PowerPoint does not have a direct feature to convert a raster image like a JPG into editable vector shapes. The 'Edit Points' and 'Ungroup' options apply to vector objects or shapes created within PowerPoint. However, you can apply various 'Artistic Effects' to your JPG to make it *look* like a drawing. Select the image, go to the 'Picture Tools' > 'Format' tab, then click 'Artistic Effects' and choose an option like 'Line Drawing' or 'Pencil Sketch'.
updated_solution: PowerPoint cannot directly convert a JPG image into editable lines and curves. The "Edit Points" feature is for existing vector shapes, not raster images. To achieve this, you'll need to use a dedicated vector graphics editor (like Inkscape or Adobe Illustrator) to trace your JPG caricature into a vector format (e.g., SVG or EMF). Once converted, insert that vector file into PowerPoint, and you should then be able to ungroup it and use "Edit Points" to modify individual lines.
accepted_answer: PowerPoint's never been able to convert raster images (like your JPG) to vector.   Raster = a collection of variously colored dots that happen to look like a picture Vector = mathematically defined lines/text/shapes that make up a picture  PowerPoint and many other programs can convert vector graphics to raster but it takes a tracing program to go the other way.  PowerPoint doesn't do it ... sorry.

title: How can I by formula extract numbers from column without the Zero's?
original_question: example: What I have                         What I need        A                                       B 1     50                                     50 2     60                                     60 3     0                                       40 4     40                                     30 5     0 6     0 7     30 Thanks

user_response: I'm not sure about that. I just know I'm using Microsoft Excel.
preliminary_solution: If you are using Excel 365 or Excel 2019+, you can use the FILTER function. In cell B1, enter the formula =FILTER(A1:A7, A1:A7<>0) and press Enter. This will automatically spill the non-zero values into subsequent cells, removing the zeros and compacting the list.
updated_solution: To extract numbers from column A without zeros, use the FILTER function. In cell B1, enter the formula =FILTER(A1:A7, A1:A7<>0). This will automatically populate column B with only the non-zero values from your specified range. Adjust the range A1:A7 to accurately reflect your actual data column.

I was able to get Desktop Back from another forum post that had a download which currently now is fine, but that not the worst part, my Change PC setting's is gone as well,when i click it nothing happens and i mean nothing if i also try changing my account picture on the metro screen as well nothing happens just....nothing. If anyone has a solution please post it, also another thing that has been happening rapidly. Every time i turn off my PC the next day i turn it on my WiFi is disconnected,if it's not then in the middle of the day it will say *WIFI NAME* Limited and i get disconnected, what does it mean and how do i fix it, I've run Virus scans with Norton and nothing has come up well something but i fixed it,anyways PLEASE HELP!!!

user_response: Yes, I checked for System Restore points. But the only ones I have are from a few days ago, and I deleted those folders months ago.
preliminary_solution: 1. Attempt a System Restore to a date before you deleted the folders. Search for "Create a restore point" and follow the prompts. 2. If no restore points are available, open Command Prompt as administrator and run "sfc /scannow" followed by "DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth". 3. Reinstall your Wi-Fi adapter drivers from your computer manufacturer's website.
updated_solution: Given the deletion of critical system folders and the lack of suitable restore points, the most effective solution is to perform a System Refresh or a clean reinstallation of Windows 8.1. This will restore all missing system components and likely resolve the issues with apps, PC settings, and the persistent WiFi disconnections. Remember to back up all your personal files before proceeding with either option.
accepted_answer: Some files can be deleted from the Temp folder, but not the folder itself because Windows uses it to hold temporary files in.  It's a bit like your car and it's gas tank. You can drain it, but you still have a fully functional automobile. But if you remove the gas tank, then you're not going to be going anywhere until you replace it.  You now find yourself in that situation with your computer. Until you replace the Temp folder and its contents, your machine is not going to function properly.  I think you're looking at having to Refresh the system in order to get it going again. What you'll have to do first is to create a Recovery disk and copy the manufacturer's partition to that. This means you'll have to go buy an external hard drive with at least 60GB free. But bearing in mind that the cost of a 60GB hard disk and one with a 500GB capacity is barely a matter of cents, you'll be better off with the large capacity drive which you can use for other purposes later.  To create the Recovery drive, hit Windows key to open the Start screen with the tiles and start typing the word "Recovery". You'll see the option called "Create a recovery drive" appear on the right. accepted_answer: You can only resize a window that is not maximized. You need to click the maximize/restore icon to restore the window. It is a toggle switch. The first screenshot shows what it looks like in a maximized window that takes up the whole screen. The second screenshot shows what it looks like if the window is not maximized. This window's borders can be dragged.     Another quick way in Windows 7 and higher to see windows side by side is to select one window, hold down the Window key on your keyboard and hit the right arrow. That will send the window to the right hand side of the screen and it will take up half of the monitor. title: Date format changes when doing a mail merge
original_question: I am using Word & Excel 2007 and am currently trying to do a mail merge.  I have a DOB field in excel and the date format throughout the excel spreadsheets are dd/mm/yy.  When i do the mail merge the DOB date appears as m/dd/yy. Any suggestions as how to change it to the dd/mm/yy format.   Thanks Keiley
clarifying_question: When you insert the DOB field into your Word document, are you inserting it directly or using any specific field codes?
user_response: I'm not sure about that. I just inserted it the regular way during the mail merge.
preliminary_solution: To correct the date format, right-click the merged date field in Word and select 'Toggle Field Codes'. Add a date-time picture switch to the field code, for example, { MERGEFIELD "DOB" \@ "dd/MM/yyyy" }. Update the field and then complete the merge.
updated_solution: To correct the date format in your mail merge, insert the DOB field into your Word document. Right-click the inserted field and select "Toggle Field Codes." Add "\@ "dd/MM/yy"" (including the quotes) after the field name, so it looks like { MERGEFIELD DOB \@ "dd/MM/yy" }. Right-click again and select "Toggle Field Codes" to see the updated format. Run the merge to confirm.
accepted_answer: Right-click the merge field. Select Toggle Field Codes from the popup menu. You'll see something like { MERGEFIELD "DOB" * MERGEFORMAT } Change the latter part so that it looks like { MERGEFIELD "DOB" @ "dd/MM/yyyy" } Press F9 to hide the field code and to update the result.

title: Object Duplicate vs. Copy & Paste - who to put the money on?
original_question: I'm trying to piece together the difference between Copy+Paste and Duplicate with respect to a Slide but more so objects. I've seen an article or two, but they mix and match duplicating in Powerpoint. Is there like a MVP written blog on the real deal. I know one is it doesn't flush the most recent Copy and Paste buffer. It seems to put it in a different position....but just when I think that's it, it isn't (they both got to the same place). Less key strokes. I'm left disappointed by blog postings such as this: http://iconlogic.blogs.com/weblog/2011/06/powerpoint-2010-whats-the-difference-between-duplicate-and-copy.html
clarifying_question: When you observe that both 'Duplicate' and 'Copy+Paste' place objects in the 'same place,' are you referring to duplicating individual objects or entire slides?
user_response: I am talking about individual objects on the slide. I haven't really been looking at copying entire slides.
preliminary_solution: When duplicating an object, PowerPoint creates a copy with a slight offset (down and to the right) from the original, and it does not use the clipboard. Copy+Paste, however, places the copied object directly on top of the original's position and utilizes the clipboard. This means you can duplicate an object and then immediately paste something else you copied previously. For slides, 'Duplicate Slide' creates an exact copy immediately after the original, while 'Copy+Paste' allows you to choose the insertion point.
updated_solution: For individual objects, Duplicate (Ctrl+D) does not use the clipboard, preserving its content, and places the new object with a consistent offset that PowerPoint remembers for subsequent duplicates. Copy+Paste (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V) uses the clipboard and typically places the object with a slight, often less predictable, offset. Duplicate is generally faster for creating multiple copies of an object within PowerPoint.
accepted_answer: To a great extent it's a subjective decision. The article Brandon referred you to provides some additional insight on the use of Duplicate. I personally prefer to use Duplicate depending on the task when working on a specific slide. Also, you can Duplicate a selection by holding Option or Control while dragging it. accepted_answer:
If a graphic is wrapped, Word automatically creates the caption in a text box; if the graphic is In Line With Text, the caption is ordinary paragraph text. If the graphics in question are not wrapped, then obviously the text boxes are unneeded. In older versions of Word, there was an option to convert the text box to a frame, and you could then select Remove Frame in the Format Frame dialog. In Word 2010, I think you'll just have to cut the text from the text box, paste it into a new paragraph (in the Caption style), and then delete the empty text box. It looks like you've got just five to do, so perhaps that won't be too onerous.

The following macro embeds all Shapes & InlineShapes in a document in tables as InlineShapes with a row for Captions. Whatever positioning applied to the original Shape object will apply to the table also. As coded, the ‘MakeImageTable’ macro limits the inserted InlineShape dimesnsions to 7.5*5.0cm. That code can, of course, be edited/omitted, in which case, so too can the corresponding code that re-sizes the inserted InlineShape to fit.  To have the caption row appear above the image, instead of below, change the Rows(1) references to Rows(2), change the Cell(1, 1) references to Cell(2, 1) and change the change the Cell(2, 1) references to Cell(1, 1).  Note that, due to the extra space taken up by the caption row, all except for the first of the document's pictures are liable to shift position slightly.  Sub AddImageCaptionTables() Dim iShp As InlineShape, Rng As Range, Tbl As Table Dim i As Long, PicWdth As Single, PicHght As Single, VPos As Single Dim HPos As Single, VRel As Long, HRel As Long, BShp As Boolean With ActiveDocument   For i = 1 To .InlineShapes.Count     If .InlineShapes(i).Range.Information(wdWithInTable) = False Then       With .InlineShapes(i)         .LockAspectRatio = True         PicWdth = .Width         PicHght = .Height         Set Rng = .Range       End With       With Rng         If .Characters.Last.Next.Text = vbCr Then .Characters.Last.Next.Text = vbNullString         .InlineShapes(1).Range.Cut       End With       BShp = False: VRel = 0: HRel = 0: VPos = 0: HPos = 0       Call MakeImageTable(Rng, PicWdth, PicHght, BShp, VRel, HRel, VPos, HPos)     End If   Next   While .Shapes.Count > 0     BShp = True     With .Shapes(1)       .LockAspectRatio = True       PicWdth = .Width       PicHght = .Height       VRel = .RelativeVerticalPosition       HRel = .RelativeHorizontalPosition       VPos = .Top       HPos = .Left       Set iShp = .ConvertToInlineShape     End With     With iShp       Set Rng = .Range       .Range.Cut     End With     Call MakeImageTable(Rng, PicWdth, PicHght, BShp, VRel, HRel, VPos, HPos)   Wend End With End Sub  Sub MakeImageTable(Rng As Range, PicWdth As Single, PicHght As Single, BShp As Boolean, _   VRel As Long, HRel As Long, VPos As Single, HPos As Single) Dim Tbl As Table, sngScl As Single, iShp As InlineShape 'Constrain maximum image size If PicWdth > CentimetersToPoints(7.5) Then   sngScl = PicWdth / CentimetersToPoints(7.5)   PicWdth = PicWdth / sngScl   PicHght = PicHght / sngScl End If If PicHght > CentimetersToPoints(5) Then   sngScl = PicHght / CentimetersToPoints(5)   PicWdth = PicWdth / sngScl   PicHght = PicHght / sngScl End If 'Create & format the table Set Tbl = Rng.Tables.Add(Range:=Rng, Numrows:=2, NumColumns:=1) With Tbl   .Borders.Enable = True   .Columns.Width = PicWdth   .TopPadding = 0   .BottomPadding = 0   .LeftPadding = 0   .RightPadding = 0   .Spacing = 0   .Rows(1).HeightRule = wdRowHeightExactly   .Rows(1).Height = PicHght   With .Rows     .LeftIndent = 0     If BShp = True Then       .WrapAroundText = True       .HorizontalPosition = HPos       .RelativeHorizontalPosition = HRel       .VerticalPosition = VPos       .RelativeVerticalPosition = VRel       .AllowOverlap = False     End If   End With   With .Cell(1, 1).Range     With .ParagraphFormat       .SpaceBefore = 0       .SpaceAfter = 0       .LeftIndent = 0       .RightIndent = 0       .FirstLineIndent = 0       .KeepWithNext = True     End With     .Paste     ' Resize the inserted InlineShape     Set iShp = .InlineShapes(1)     With iShp       .Width = PicWdth       .Height = PicHght     End With   End With   With .Cell(2, 1).Range     .Style = "Caption"     .End = .End - 1     .InsertAfter vbCr     .InsertCaption Label:="Figure", TitleAutoText:=" ", Title:="", _       Position:=wdCaptionPositionBelow, ExcludeLabel:=0     .Paragraphs.First.Range.Characters.Last.Text = vbNullString     .Borders(wdBorderLeft).LineStyle = wdLineStyleNone     .Borders(wdBorderRight).LineStyle = wdLineStyleNone     .Borders(wdBorderTop).LineStyle = wdLineStyleNone   End With End With End Sub  To work with just the selected image (or multiple images in a selected range), change: With ActiveDocument to: With Selection and change:   While .Shapes.Count > 0     With .Shapes(1) to:   While .ShapeRange.Count > 0     With .ShapeRange(1)

title: Formula stopped working
original_question: I have a spreadsheet that I have been using for ten months, I simply place the data in the correct columns, and the COUNTIF formulas calculate how many people of a certain profession and pay grade exists. This has worked for ten months, today I go to execute the same thing I have always done and it will not work. Interestingly enough, it work for the first 80% of the data in the worksheet...my mind is blown!    I have attempted to repaste in to the various spreadsheets I've saved over the last ten months, I've tried erasing all of the data and re-inputting, I've checked to make sure the Automatic calculation is on, I have ensured I don't have a circular formula, and I've tried changing the formatting from General to Text...nothing has worked.
clarifying_question: When the COUNTIF formula 'will not work' for the last 20% of your data, what specific value or error does it display (e.g., 0, #VALUE!, #N/A)?
user_response: I'm not sure about that. I just know it fails to calculate correctly for the remaining data.
preliminary_solution: 1. Verify Formula Range: Double-check that the range referenced in your COUNTIF formulas extends to include all the new data rows. If not, adjust the range to cover the entire dataset. 2. Clean Data: For the problematic data in the last 20%, use the TRIM function (e.g., =TRIM(A1)) to remove any leading/trailing spaces, and ensure there are no hidden characters that might prevent a match. 3. Data Type Check: Confirm that the data in the criteria and the cells being counted are consistently formatted as either text or numbers.
updated_solution: The problem likely lies with subtle data inconsistencies in the last 20% of your data. First, check for extra spaces or non-printable characters in the cells that aren't counting correctly. Use the TRIM() and CLEAN() functions in a helper column to normalize the text data. Second, carefully verify that your COUNTIF formula's range correctly extends to include all the problematic data. Finally, ensure your COUNTIF criteria exactly matches the normalized data, as COUNTIF requires precise matches.
accepted_answer: Check the data for invisible leading or trailing blanks.  You could use the Len() function to compare what you see with what the cell contains. Or you could use   =SEARCH(" ",A1)  and copy down. If it returns a number, there is a blank in the cell. Also, if the data is copied from a web site, it may contain the non-breaking space, which is different from a regular space. To check for these, use  =SEARCH(CHAR(160),A1)

title: How to find same words in excel
original_question: Hello  I want to find same words in excel and also same meaning words like  Example 1 : same words Global Chocolate Caramels Market Professional Survey Report 2017 Global Chocolate Caramels Market Professional Survey Report 2017   Example 2 : same meaning words like Global Chocolate Powdered Drinks Market Professional Survey Report 2017 Global Chocolate Powdered Drink Market Professional Survey Report 2017  We have thousands of titles like this in excel sheet i want to identify like above title in sheet.
clarifying_question: When you say 'same meaning words,' are you looking for near-exact matches with minor variations (like singular/plural or slight rephrasing), or do you need to identify titles that convey the same general topic even if the wording is significantly different?
user_response: I am looking for near-exact matches with minor variations, like singular versus plural words. For example, one title might say 'Drinks' and another says 'Drink', but the rest of the title is exactly the same.
preliminary_solution: 1. To identify exact duplicate titles, select your data range and apply Conditional Formatting > Highlight Cell Rules > Duplicate Values. 2. For titles with 'same meaning words' that have minor variations (e.g., singular/plural differences), create a helper column. In this column, use formulas to standardize the text (e.g., convert to lowercase, remove punctuation, or normalize common word endings). Then, apply Conditional Formatting for duplicate values to this helper column.
updated_solution: 1. To find exact duplicates, select your data range, go to Home > Conditional Formatting > Highlight Cells Rules > Duplicate Values. 2. For near-exact matches with singular/plural variations, create a helper column. In this column, use nested SUBSTITUTE functions to normalize common plural words (e.g., =SUBSTITUTE(SUBSTITUTE(A1,"Drinks","Drink"),"Reports","Report")). Apply Conditional Formatting for Duplicate Values to this helper column to identify these matches.
